OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S DEMURER TO PLTF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF FILED BY PLAINTIFF ENGBERG, EDWIN AN INDIVIDUAL MANIBUSAN, MIKE AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT AKAL SECURITY, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED. THE EIGHTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS ADEQUATELY PLED. HOWEVER, MR. MANIBUSAN MAY ONLY RECOVER EMOTIONAL DISTRESS DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. TO THE EXTENT MT. MANIBUSAN PURPORTS TO ALLEGE A CLAIM DIRECTED AT THE INTENTIONAL, MALICIOUS ASPECTS OF DEFENDANTS' CONDUCT APART FROM THE VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY, THAT CLAIM IS BARRED AS COVERED BY THE WORKERS' COMPENSATION EXCLUSIVITY PROVISIONS. (SHOEMAKER V. MYERS (1990) 52 CAL.3D 1, 25.) PLAINTIFFS ARE NOTIFIED THAT THEIR FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT HAS NOT BEEN FILED. THE MARCH 8, 2012 ORDER PERMITTING THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT TO BE FILED DID NOT INCLUDE THE PROPOSED LANGUAGE DEEMING IT FILED. PLAINTIFFS MUST IMMEDIATELY FILE THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE PARTIES WERE ORDERED TO MEET AND CONFER TO TRY AND RESOLVE THEIR CONFLICTS, WHICH WAS UNSUCCESSFUL. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AFTER HEARING, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT AKAL SECURITY, INC.'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ANSWERS TO DEPOSTION QUESTIONS AND TO KEEP DISCOVERY OPEN IS DENIED. ON JANUARY 10, 2013, SINCE THE BRIEFING ON THIS MOTION, PLAINTIFF HAS DISMISSED HIS CLAIM FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. IN LIGHT OF THAT, THE DEPOSITION QUESTIONS ARE IRRELEVANT AND/OR INFRINGE UPON THE PRIVACY INTERESTS OF THE NON-PARTY DEPONENT. REQUESTS FOR COSTS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.

Mtn For Order Compelling Answers To Depo Questions And To Keep Disco Open
Off Calendar Jan-25-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ellen P. Rosenbluth, Esq. of Frankel Goldware LLP, Tel. 925.355.9800, for defendant and moving party;
Christopher R. LeClerc, Esq. of Le Clerc & Le Clerc LLP, Tel. 415.445.0900, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The parties were ordered to meet and confer to try and resolve their conflicts, which was unsuccessful. The matter was argued. After hearing, the court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT AKAL SECURITY, INC.'S Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Depostion Questions And To Keep Discovery Open is denied. On January 10, 2013, since the briefing on this motion, Plaintiff has dismissed his claim for intentional infliction of emotional distress. In light of that, the deposition questions are irrelevant and/or infringe upon the privacy interests of the non-party deponent. Requests for costs denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AKAL SECURITY, INC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF.
Appearances: Ellen P. Rosenbluth of Frankel & Goldware, LLP (925.355.9800) for defendant and moving party; Stuart G. Gross of Gross Law (671.4628) for plaintiff and responding party.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Overruled. The eighth cause of action is adequately pled. However, Mr. Manibusan may only recover emotional distress damages arising out of Defendant's alleged violation of public policy. To the extent Mt. Manibusan purports to allege a claim directed at the intentional, malicious aspects of defendants' conduct apart from the violation of public policy, that claim is barred as covered by the workers' compensation exclusivity provisions. (Shoemaker v. Myers (1990) 52 Cal.3d 1, 25.) Plaintiffs are notified that their First Amended Complaint has not been filed. The March 8, 2012 Order permitting the First Amended Complaint to be filed did not include the proposed language deeming it filed. Plaintiffs must immediately file the First Amended Complaint. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.
